body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #57895 by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #57895 added new **convenience generators** on `ResponseEventStream` тАФ a middle ground between `TextResponse` (simple text-only) and the full builder API (`AddOutputItemMessage()` etc.): - `stream.OutputItemMessage(string text)` тАФ emits a complete text message (all inner events) in a single call - `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` тАФ streams token-by-token with each chunk becoming a `response.output_text.delta` event - `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` тАФ emits a complete function call output item in one call - `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` тАФ emits a reasoning output item The samples (Sample 3, 4, 6) were restructured to lead with convenience-first patterns, but the **main README** and **CHANGELOG** were not updated to reflect these new APIs. ## Gaps Found - The main `README.md` "Key concepts" section documents only `TextResponse` and the full builder control pattern (`AddOutputItemMessage()` / `AddTextContent()` / `EmitAdded()` etc.) тАФ the convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`, `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall()`, `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem()`) are invisible to a developer reading only the README - The CHANGELOG (`CHANGELOG.md`) has only the initial release entry and does not mention any of the convenience generators added in PR #57895 - The README description of `ResponseEventStream` should clarify when to use convenience generators vs. the full builder API (currently missing entirely from the key concepts section)
ЁЯУР Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` тАФ EDIT In the **Key concepts тЖТ ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream тАФ full control over every SSE event:` subsection, add a new subsection documenting convenience generators. Replace the current `ResponseEventStream` description block (approximately lines 57тАУ81) with an expanded version: ```markdown **`ResponseEventStream` тАФ convenience generators (recommended for most cases):** When `TextResponse` is too simple but the full builder API is more than you need, use the convenience generators on `ResponseEventStream`. They handle all inner events (`output_item.added`, `content_part.added`, deltas, `content_part.done`, `output_item.done`) automatically: ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await Task.CompletedTask; var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); // One call emits all text output events automatically. var input = request.GetInputText(); foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($"Hello! You said: \"{input}\"")) yield return evt; yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); } } ``` For token-by-token streaming, pass an `IAsyncEnumerable`: ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_StreamingConvenienceHandler await foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage(tokenSourceAsync, cancellationToken)) yield return evt; ``` For function calling, use `OutputItemFunctionCall()`: ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_FunctionCallConvenience foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemFunctionCall("get_weather", "call_1", arguments)) yield return evt; ``` **`ResponseEventStream` тАФ full builder control:** Use the builder API only when you need to interleave non-event work *between individual delta/done calls within a content part*, or set custom properties on an output item before `EmitAdded()`: ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl // ... (existing code) ... ``` ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md` тАФ EDIT Add a new entry under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `### Features Added`: ```markdown - Added `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call: - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` тАФ emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically) - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` тАФ streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` тАФ emits a complete function call output item - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` тАФ emits a reasoning output item - Samples restructured for progressive complexity: convenience-first patterns in Samples 3, 4, and 6 with full builder variants for advanced use cases. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The convenience generator snippets referenced in Step 1 need corresponding `#region` / `#endregion` blocks in a sample test file. 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample0_ReadMe.cs` (create if it doesn't exist, or add to an existing ReadMe sample file) For each snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_StreamingConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_FunctionCallConvenience`), wrap the handler class or usage snippet with: ```csharp #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler // ... handler code ... #endregion ``` 2. **Add snippet placeholders in README.md** at the exact locations described in Step 1: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler ``` ```` 3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to inject the actual code. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already has all required sections: 1. тЬЕ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. тЬЕ Key concepts тАФ **INCOMPLETE**: missing convenience generator documentation 3. тЬЕ Examples (links to samples) 4. тЬЕ Troubleshooting 5. тЬЕ Next steps 6. тЬЕ Contributing Only the **Key concepts** section needs updating as described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` тАФ injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` тАФ regenerates public API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document new ResponseEventStream convenience generators in README and CHANGELOG
